title: GrowGeneration Corp Q4 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: GrowGeneration reported strong 2021 results with revenue of $422 million, representing a 118% increase from $193 million in 2020. The company achieved 24% same-store sales growth and expanded to 63 locations across 13 states. Full-year net income was $12.8 million ($0.21 per share). Gross profit margins improved to 28% from 26.4% in the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA reached $34.5 million. CEO Darren Lampert acknowledged significant headwinds affecting the industry, stating that the company has seen a slowdown in LED lighting and capex projects coming out of the third quarter 2021 into 2022. The company cited cannabis oversupply in western states and delayed legalization timelines as primary pressures. For 2022, management outlined five core priorities: opening 15-20 greenfield locations in emerging states, technology investments for operational efficiency, establishing five regional distribution centers, expanding proprietary brands and private label revenue, and consolidating e-commerce platforms. The company projects full-year revenue between $415-445 million and adjusted EBITDA of $30-35 million, reflecting anticipated market challenges, particularly in Q1.
